title: ws.rs WHISPER has no rate limit — DB-amplified offline queue flood
description: WHISPER handler (line 1312 ws.rs) calls user_exists + enqueue with no check_rate_limit. SEALED_BROADCAST, GROUP_SEND, and every other write handler all call check_rate_limit; WHISPER was missed. A single authenticated client can send WHISPER at the 1 MiB/frame WebSocket cap speed, forcing a user_exists DB query + an enqueue DB write + O(1 offline queue insert) per frame until the 1000-message cap is hit. At 60 req/min (the relay default) this is tolerable, but without the rate limit it is unbounded. The enqueue DROP at 1000 is a flood cap, not a rate limit — it does not protect the DB write throughput. Trigger: attacker calls WHISPER in a tight loop after auth. Bad behavior: relay SQLite pool saturated; legitimate operations queued behind flood.
